This substitution improves the durability and strength of the apatite crystal under acidic environments, such as in the mouth where acids from bacteria cause demineralization. \boxed{A} Based on the evidence, fluoride replaces the hydroxyl ion (OH⁻) in hydroxyapatite to form fluorapatite, which has improved resistance to acid dissolution and thus greater strength. Thus, the correct answer is $\boxed{\text{A}}$.
